name: Oyster and Mushroom Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 cup half and half, or heavy cream
1 cup soft bread crumbs, (2 slices bread)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 425°F.
2. Drain the oysters, saving the liquor.
3. Combine the oyster liquor with enough half and half to make 1-1/2 cups. Set aside.
4. Melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a heavy 9 or 10 inch skillet over moderate heat. Add the mushrooms, and cook, uncovered for about 3 minutes, or until lightly browned. Remove the mushrooms to a platter and set aside.
5. Reduce the heat to low and melt 1 tablespoon of the remaining butter in the skillet. Blend in the flour, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly. Gradually add the oyster liquor and half and half mixture, and cook, stirring constantly until the mixture thickens -- about 3 minutes. Remove from the heat.
6. Add the oysters, the cooked mushrooms and any accumulated liquid, and the Worcestershire sauce, lemon juice, cayenne pepper, salt and black pepper.
7. Pour the mixture into a buttered 8-inch glass or ceramic pie plate.
8. Sprinkle with the bread crumbs, and dot with the last tablespoon of butter.
9. Bake, uncovered, until the crumbs are browned -- about 10 minutes.
